conversations.content: For repositories owned by me, remove my stars if they have also been starred by other users.

<function_thought>
To accomplish the task of removing stars from repositories owned by the user, I first need to gather information about the logged-in GitHub user, including their repositories. Then, I'll check each repository to identify which ones are starred by other users. Finally, I will proceed to unstar those repositories. The first function to call is `get_logged_in_github_user_information` to get the user's details. 
</function_thought>

<function_call>[get_logged_in_github_user_information()]</function_call>

<final_answer></final_answer>

<function_thought>
I have retrieved the necessary information about the logged-in user, Emma Johnson. She owns the following repositories: `file_compression`, `image_transformation`, and `linear_algebra_operation`. The repository currently starred by her is `file_compression`. Next, I will check if any of her repositories are starred by other users. Since there are no other starred repositories listed, I will assume the starred status for checking and proceed to retrieve information about each repository to find out if they are starred by others.
</function_thought>

<function_call>[get_github_repository_information(repo_name='emmajohnson/file_compression'), get_github_repository_information(repo_name='emmajohnson/image_transformation'), get_github_repository_information(repo_name='emmajohnson/linear_algebra_operation')]</function_call>
